message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "claim with negative damage amount → throws"
Current error: expected function to throw, but it doesn't — handleClaim accepts negative damage.amount silently
Passing tests so far: 28

The new test (around line 488-505) quotes a sword, then submits a claim where damages contains `{itemType: "sword", amount: -200}`. The implementation must throw when a damage amount is negative.

Implement the minimal change in src/claim-office.ts so this test passes. Run `pnpm test` to confirm all 29 tests pass.

Do NOT refactor — that comes in the next phase.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal change to make the failing test pass.

content.text:
Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added a minimal guard at the start of `computeDamagePayout` that throws when `damage.amount < 0`.

**Result**: All 29 tests pass (1 todo remaining).

**Approach**: This is the minimal change - a single conditional check at the entry point of damage payout computation. The throw happens before any other processing, satisfying the test's expectation that a claim with a negative damage amount throws. No refactoring was done.

File modified: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-57-53_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
